name: Aichi Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace, and the bright future of the prefecture

description: A wine-red field charged at the centre with a large white stylised hiragana あ (a), the first kana of the prefecture's name.
history: Aichi's prefectural symbol was adopted on 13 November 1950, after a public design competition. The flag, with the white symbol on a wine-red field, has been used as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: the deep wine-red ground of the prefectural flag

a stylised hiragana あ (a), the first character of Aichi (あいち / 愛知); the rounded, swirling form is also read as a wave on the sea, evoking Aichi's Pacific coast and its modern industry and development.
